title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Client Identity, and Proxy Trust · Execution Trace: Request Identity Redaction
summary: An origin accepts arbitrary Host values, constructs password-reset links from untrusted forwarded fields, rate-limits a proxy address, and logs raw targets containing secrets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo request identity redaction increment in request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record low-cardinality route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data.
Parse query values, duplicate keys, empty values, encoding, canonicalization, and sensitive parameters without rebuilding ambiguous URLs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Request Identity Redaction · Theory Request Identity Redaction
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4608 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive effective authority only through an explicit trusted-proxy boundary; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record low-cardinality route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data.
